items.title: Veteran journalist Joshua Purushotman remembered for steady hand, sharp instincts, quick wit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: He was the steady hand in hectic newsrooms and the voice of clarity in public relations — colleagues remember Joshua Purushotman as the consummate journalist who combined sharp instincts with easy wit. David Christy, who worked alongside Purushotman at the New Straits Times, described him as inquisitive and friendly. "When he joined us, and even long after that, he would constantly ask me questions about work," said David, who is NST's executive editor for Production.

items.title: Mercy Malaysia official describes escalations in Lebanon ahead of truce
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mercy Malaysia head of programme operations Shah Fiesal Hussain recalled how strikes in Lebanon intensified in the days leading up to a 10-point ceasefire plan between the United States and Iran. He described the situation on April 5 in Beirut as one of the most violent escalations of the conflict. Lebanon's Health Ministry said at least 203 people were killed and 1,000 others wounded in air strikes that targeted areas in Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, Mount Lebanon, Sidon and several villages in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Sustained net inflow of foreign funds signals long-term stability
items.summary: Economists said consistent inflows would underpin business activity, deepen market liquidity and strengthen overall financial conditions. In the first quarter of this year, total foreign portfolio inflows reached RM5.8 billion, supported by net inflows into both debt markets of RM4.6 billion and equities of RM1.2 billion. Idham said geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices could reverse or slow capital inflows.

items.title: Supply tightening boosts value for Malaysian palm oil
items.summary: The country's palm oil stocks tumbled 16.1 per cent to 2.27 million tonnes in March from February, marking their lowest level since August, data from the Malaysian Palm Oil Board showed on Friday. Industry observers said this tightening supply, down for a third consecutive month, demonstrates the global market's strong appetite for Malaysian palm oil. Crude palm oil production increased 7.2 per cent to 1.38 million tonnes, snapping a four-month run of declines.

items.title: Tanjung Sepat residents at odds over calls for shutdown of pig farms
items.summary: The directive follows longstanding concerns over odour, environmental management and land use involving pig farms in parts of Selangor. State authorities have said enforcement and restructuring efforts are aimed at ensuring stricter compliance with environmental regulations, including centralised farming systems and proper waste treatment. Some said odour from nearby farms was occasional rather than constant.

items.title: Pig farms pose biggest threat to river health in Selangor, say environmentalists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pig farming is among the biggest threats to rivers in Selangor as rapid industrial growth and weak law enforcement strain water resources in Malaysia's most developed state, environmentalists warned. Association of Water and Energy Research Malaysia president S. Piarapakaran said rivers and clean water are "the backbone of daily life and industrialisation", with semiconductor and manufacturing industries requiring vast volumes of clean water. Experts estimate 81 per cent of Malaysia's drinking water comes from rivers, underlining the high stakes.

items.title: Iranian delegation in Pakistan for talks with US, Vance on his way
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Iran's delegation arrived in Pakistan on Friday for ceasefire negotiations with the United States as Teheran insisted on a truce in Lebanon and release of its blocked assets for the talks to go ahead. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said making progress would be hard work. Iranian state television said the delegation was led by parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and reiterated Iran's position that talks would only begin if Washington accepts its preconditions – a Lebanon ceasefire and the unfreezing of Iran's assets.

items.title: Ready to move, but where? Selangor pig farmers seek clarity on relocation
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT: Pig farmers, while willing to comply with the sultan of Selangor's call for the cessation of pig farming in the state, are grappling with a critical uncertainty — where they can relocate. Third-generation farmer P. Kartigeyan, whose farm in Tanjung Sepat once supplied pigs for export to Singapore, said the situation was deeply unsettling. My father expanded it, and now I'm running operations," he said.

items.title: RON95 at RM1.99, tolls frozen: Where subsidies still cushion Malaysians
items.summary: Not all subsidies have disappeared. RON95 at RM1.99 still anchors cost-of-living support Fuel remains the most significant subsidy affecting everyday life. Support measures linked to fuel subsidies have also been recalibrated.

items.title: Trump's peace board faces cash crunch, stalling Gaza plan, sources say
items.summary: JERUSALEM/CAIRO: Donald Trump's Board of Peace has received only a tiny fraction of the US$17 billion pledged for Gaza, preventing the US president from pushing ahead with his plan for the shattered Palestinian enclave's future, sources told Reuters. The plan envisages large-scale rebuilding of the coastal enclave after the disarmament of Palestinian militant group Hamas – whose attacks on Israel triggered the assault on Gaza – and the withdrawal of Israeli troops. The funding pledges were also meant to pay for the activities of a nascent National Committee for the Administration of Gaza (NCAG), a US-backed group of Palestinian technocrats intended to assume control of Gaza from Hamas.

items.title: Five things to know about the planned Iran-US talks in Islamabad
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan is set to host talks between Iran and the United States in a bid to turn a fragile two-week ceasefire into a lasting end to a war that has left thousands dead and roiled global energy markets. Here are five things to know about the Islamabad talks: The war behind the talks On February 28, the United States and Israel launched deadly coordinated strikes that killed supreme leader Ali Khamenei and struck Iran's military and nuclear infrastructure. On Apr 8, Washington and Teheran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan.

items.title: Trump says Iran has 'no cards' beyond Hormuz control
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said Friday that Iran has "no cards" in upcoming talks with the United States – apart from Teheran's effective stranglehold on the crucial Strait of Hormuz shipping channel. "The Iranians don't seem to realise they have no cards, other than a short-term extortion of the world by using international waterways," Trump said on his Truth Social network. Separately on Friday, Trump told the New York Post that US warships are being reloaded with weaponry to strike Iran if the talks fail to produce a deal.

items.title: Pakistan PM says US, Iran leadership both attending Islamabad talks
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said Friday that leaders of both Iran and the United States would attend ceasefire talks in his country – and warned that progress would be hard work. There, negotiations will be held for the establishment of peace," Sharif said in an address to the nation. He said his government would "make every possible effort to make these talks successful."

items.title: Israel won't discuss ceasefire with Hizbollah in Lebanon talks, says envoy
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Israel said Friday it will not discuss a ceasefire with Hizbollah when it holds talks with Lebanon's government next week in Washington. Israel "agreed to begin formal peace negotiations" with the Lebanese government, with which it has no diplomatic relations, said the Israeli ambassador, Yechiel Leiter. "Israel refused to discuss a ceasefire with the Hizbollah terrorist organisation, which continues to attack Israel and is the main obstacle to peace between the two countries," he said in a statement.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts hurtle home from moon towards splashdown
items.summary: HOUSTON: The four Artemis II astronauts, returning from the world's first crewed moon voyage in over half a century, hurtled back towards Earth on Friday as they prepared their Orion spacecraft for the final phase of their descent and splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off Southern California. On Friday afternoon, the autonomously piloted Orion crew capsule executed one last eight-second firing of its jet thrusters to fine-tune the flight course, a critical manoeuvre to ensure a safe return. NASA's celebrated 10-day mission was expected to culminate with the gumdrop-shaped Orion vehicle jettisoning the service module housing its main rocket system, followed by a fiery re-entry through Earth's atmosphere and a six-minute radio blackout before the capsule parachutes into the sea.

items.title: Designs unveiled for Trump's proposed 250-foot arch in Washington
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Designs were unveiled on Friday for a 250-foot (76-metre) arch in Washington sought by US President Donald Trump that, if approved, would reshape the capital's monument-filled skyline. Trump administration officials formally submitted detailed renderings of the proposed "Triumphal Arch" ahead of a meeting next week to advance the project that Trump has touted for months. The designs call for an ivory-coloured arch modelled on the Roman Arch of Titus from classical antiquity and resembling the Arc de Triomphe in Paris.

items.title: Trump warns of fresh strikes if Iran talks fail: NY Post
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said Friday that US warships are being reloaded with weaponry to strike Iran if talks in Pakistan fail to produce a deal, in an interview with the New York Post. We're loading up the ships with the best ammunition, the best weapons ever made – even better than what we did previously and we blew them apart," the Post quoted Trump as saying. - AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Sabah leaders, NGOs urge federal govt to honour 40pct revenue entitlement
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: About 200 people representing 19 non-governmental organisations (NGOs) and political leaders gathered at Padang Merdeka here tonight, urging the Federal Government to honour Sabah's constitutional right to 40 per cent of revenue collected from the state. The peaceful gathering, held under the Justice4Sabah 40 Per Cent Rights movement, was attended by several leaders, including Tuaran member of parliament Madius Tangau, Ranau member of parliament Jonathan Yasin, Keningau member of parliament Jeffrey Kitingan, Moyog assemblyman Donald Mojuntin and appointed Sabah assemblyman Roger Chin. He also said the government would not appeal the Kota Kinabalu High Court decision dated Oct 17 last year, while the Attorney-General's Chambers later confirmed on Nov 11 that negotiations would be held between the federal government and Sabah.

items.title: Police to intensify operation against drunk drivers, focusing on weekends
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) has directed all state contingents to intensify operations against drunk drivers, with a particular focus on weekend nights to reduce the risk of road accidents. Bukit Aman TEID director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the directive called for more frequent and aggressive nationwide enforcement under Op Mabuk. These operations are not limited to weekend nights but will also be carried out on normal nights, although weekends remain the main focus," he said.

items.title: DeepSeek's V4 model to test China's AI ambitions
items.summary: FOR weeks now, the global tech industry has been waiting for a major artificial intelligence (AI) launch from DeepSeek, seen as a benchmark for China's progress in the fast-moving field. "It's important to know because at one level, it is a signal of China's AI self-sufficiency trajectory," said Wei Sun, principal AI analyst at Counterpoint Research. Tech news outlet The Information reported last week that V4 could be run on the latest chips made by China's Huawei.

items.title: Police: Autistic girl likely left home before drowning in retention pond
items.summary: JITRA: An autistic girl who was reported missing from the People's Housing Project (PPR) at Taman Nagasari, Kepala Batas, earlier today before being found drowned in a retention pond in the afternoon is believed to have left her home on her own through the gate. Kubang Pasu district police chief, Superintendent Mohd Radzi Abdul Rahim, said initial investigations found that the gate was believed to have been opened by the victim's nine-year-old autistic brother. Focus areas included the retention pond, large drains and other high-risk locations around Taman Nagasari.

items.title: Melania's Epstein comments mark rare public foray for 'unknowable' first lady
items.summary: MELANIA Trump, an enigmatic former model from Slovenia, has occupied the role of US first lady on her own terms. Intensely private for such a high-profile figure, Melania Trump made only eight speeches in her husband's first year in office in 2017. By comparison, Michelle Obama made 74 in 2009, the first year of her husband Barack Obama's presidency.

items.title: Refined fuel prices retreat in Asia, but still show supply stress
items.summary: Global benchmark Brent crude futures ended at US$94.75 a barrel on Wednesday, down 13.3 per cent from the previous close. Brent ended at US$72.48 on Feb 27, meaning it has gained 30.7 per cent from the start of the Iran conflict. Singapore jet fuel ended at US$193.53 a barrel on Wednesday, down 14.2 per cent from its previous close and 20 per cent below the record high of US$242.06 reached on March 30.

items.title: KJ will accept Umno decision on rejoin bid, says it is too early for politics
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin says he will accept whatever decision is made by the party's Supreme Council regarding his application to rejoin Umno. Earlier, several former Umno leaders, including Khairy and Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein, were reported to have submitted applications to rejoin the party last month. Hishammuddin; former Umno information chief Shahril Sufian Hamdan; Jempol Umno division chief Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Sharif; and former Tebrau Umno division chief Datuk Maulizan Bujang were previously suspended for six years.

items.title: Israeli strike in south Lebanon kills 10 security personnel
items.summary: BEIRUT: An Israeli strike, one of multiple on the southern Lebanese city of Nabatiyeh on Friday, killed at least 10 State Security personnel, a source from the government agency told AFP, as Lebanon prepared to start ceasefire talks with Israel. Naim Qassem, head of Iran-backed party and armed group Hizbollah, called on the Lebanese state to stop giving "free concessions" to Israel, with the two governments due to begin negotiations next week to end the war that has left nearly 1,900 people in Lebanon dead. The government banned Hizbollah's military activities at the beginning of the latest war with Israel in March and is moving towards bilateral negotiations with Israel despite the opposition of Hizbollah, which is represented in the cabinet and parliament.

items.title: Ex-EC chairman Mohd Hashim Abdullah laid to rest
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Former Election Commission (EC) chairman Tan Sri Mohd Hashim Abdullah was laid to rest at the Precinct 20 Muslim Cemetery here at 10.20pm tonight. About 150 people, including family members, friends and EC staff, attended the funeral. Among those present to pay their last respects were Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah, Putrajaya MP Datuk Dr Radzi Jidin, former Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Economy) Datuk Seri Mustapa Mohamed, former senior deputy secretary-general (Prime Minister's Department) Tan Sri Othman Mahmood and former Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Mohd Irwan Serigar Abdullah.

items.title: NST Leader: How to give people access to justice
items.summary: MALAYSIA Mediation Centre's (MMC) initiative to offer free legal assistance to the B40 and M40 groups could mark a turning point in making justice accessible, affordable and efficient, especially for the middle majority often excluded from legal recourse. Here is why MMC's execution of free legal assistance is important. In Kekatong Sdn Bhd v Danaharta Urus Sdn Bhd, the Court of Appeal declared thus: Access to justice is an integral part of Article 8(1) of the Federal Constitution.

items.title: Man gets six months' jail for possession of child, adult pornographic material
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A 22-year-old man was sentenced to six months' imprisonment by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to possessing more than 8,000 pornographic files involving adults and children on his mobile phone and electronic devices. According to the charge, the accused was found in possession of child sexual abuse material in the form of videos and obscene images involving children stored in his mobile phone. She also requested that the court order the accused to undergo counselling after serving his sentence.

items.title: Blogger, social activist Din Merican dies at 87
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Blogger and long-time social activist Din Merican, a prominent voice on governance, democracy and institutional reform, has died at the age of 87. Widely known as "The Malaysian DJ Blogger", Din built a reputation for his forthright commentary on national affairs, often using his platform to call for greater accountability in public institutions, stronger democratic practices and an end to corruption. Born in Alor Star, Kedah, Din began his education at Penang Free School before reading economics at Universiti Malaya.

items.title: Cloud seeding efforts in southern peninsula to be intensified – Zahid
items.summary: MELAKA: Efforts to induce rainfall through cloud seeding in southern Peninsular Malaysia, particularly in Melaka, will be intensified with additional follow-up operations after initial results proved less encouraging. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the earlier cloud seeding exercise in Melaka had limited impact due to wind conditions, which caused rain-bearing clouds to drift away from targeted areas. Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said further attempts would be carried out to enhance rainfall and improve water levels in the state's dams.

items.title: Road fatalities jump 17pct during recent festive seasons despite police enforcement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Road fatalities rose by nearly 17 per cent during this year's Chinese New Year and Hari Raya Aidilfitri festive seasons under Op Selamat, despite enhanced enforcement efforts, Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said. Mohd Khalid said a total of 90 fatal accidents were recorded during Op Selamat 25 and 26, compared with 77 cases in previous similar operations. Op Selamat 25 was conducted in conjunction with Chinese New Year, while Op Selamat 26 covered the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period.

items.title: Scorching weather drives Kelantan hotels and homestays to solar power to cut rising costs
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 10 — The prolonged hot spell is not only affecting the public but also increasing operating costs for hotel and homestay operators in Kelantan, prompting a shift to solar energy as a long-term cost-saving measure. Operators are now actively implementing various austerity measures, including adopting green technology, following rising utility bills and increasingly challenging weather conditions. Citra Kadok Hotel & Banquet Hall managing director Masdiyah Jemain said using solar energy has become a primary approach to reducing dependency on conventional electricity.

items.title: Across borders and war zones, a Palestinian’s journey to deliver hope in Malaysia’s Gaza mission
items.summary: CAIRO, April 10 — For Mahmoud Ghneim, a Palestinian, distance and exhaustion are mere footnotes in his unwavering mission to serve his homeland. As head of the project department for Cinta Gaza Malaysia (CGM) in Gaza, Mahmoud undertook a gruelling journey of nearly two days across borders to reach the coordination hub in Egypt for the 30-container humanitarian mission to Gaza. Displaced from Gaza to Iraq, the 35-year-old did not hesitate when called to support the mission.

items.title: MACC receives report on alleged suspicious RM500,000 transaction involving former minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has confirmed it received a report alleging suspicious transactions involving a former minister linked to a vending machine project. MACC said it received an official complaint from the Sekretariat Gempur Media Sosial Rakyat (SekPur) over alleged questionable transactions amounting to RM500,000. MACC Strategic Communications Division director Hisyam Mohd Yusoff said SekPur Political Bureau chairman Shahbudin Embun, accompanied by an individual identified as Major Razali Zakaria, lodged the report and submitted related documents at about 12.30pm today.

items.title: Loan shark syndicate targeting hawkers, traders busted in Penang and KL
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 10 — Police have busted an unlicensed money lending syndicate (Ah Long) in Penang and Kuala Lumpur following the arrest of five local men in a raid in the city yesterday. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said all the men, aged 19 to 29, were arrested by a team of police from the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CID) of Penang Contingent based on information and intelligence through Ops Vulture. Azizee said that in addition, his party also seized four vehicles, namely a Mercedes, a Toyota Alphard a Perodua Myvi, and a Perodua Viva.

items.title: Ambulance crash near USM goes viral after Axia driver allegedly fails to give way
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 10 — An ambulance responding to an emergency was involved in a collision with two other vehicles on Jalan Bukit Gambir near Universiti Sains Malaysia yesterday, with footage of the incident later going viral on social media. Deputy Timur Laut police chief Supt Lee Swee Sake said a report on the accident was received at 1.06 pm. The 51-year-old ambulance driver and three medical personnel, aged between 35 and 41, sustained minor injuries.

items.title: Man arrested for drunk driving against traffic on Sprint Highway in Petaling Jaya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Police have arrested a man for driving against the flow of traffic while drunk at Kilometre 0.7 of the Sprint Highway in Petaling Jaya here early yesterday morning. Petaling Jaya district police chief said that the man was arrested by a team from the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Division at 2.10 am. He urged those with information about the incident to contact the nearest police station or traffic investigating officer, Insp M. Sivam at 017-3290749 of the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters at 03-79662222.

items.title: Mastermind among 27 held in Immigration Dept's anti-human trafficking operation in Kota Bharu
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The Immigration Department arrested 27 individuals, aged 13 to 42, in a special anti-human trafficking operation around Kota Bharu on April 8. Its director-general, Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the operation first arrested two local men, which led to a raid at a residential premises believed to be used as a safehouse and arrested 25 others, including the mastermind of the human trafficking operations. The 22 others arrested were two other local men, believed to be transporters, and 22 foreigners - eight Myanmar men and nine Myanmar women, three Bangladeshi men and two male Indian nationals.

items.title: Anwar backs Iran’s right to self-defence, declines to weigh in on Singapore minister’s Strait of Hormuz remarks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reiterated Malaysia’s support for Iran’s right to defend itself against US-Israeli attacks, while declining to weigh in on the domestic backlash against Singapore Foreign Minister Vivian Balakrishnan’s controversial statement. Several Malaysian political leaders had criticised Vivian, who recently said the republic would not negotiate for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Roughly 38 per cent of Malaysia’s crude oil imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: EPF warns Akaun Fleksibel withdrawals could weaken long-term retirement savings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The growing use of the Employees Provident Fund’s (EPF) Akaun Fleksibel facility for short-term financial needs could pose risks to long-term retirement adequacy, the pension fund has cautioned. In its latest report, “Enduring Today, Shaping Tomorrow: Akaun Fleksibel as a Financial Lifeline for Wellbeing”, the EPF said while the facility has provided immediate relief to millions of members, continued reliance on withdrawals could gradually reduce retirement savings if not carefully managed. The EPF said withdrawals are strongly linked to improved short-term wellbeing, with members reporting greater financial security after accessing their funds, particularly when it helps them manage urgent expenses or stay afloat amid rising costs.

items.title: PM Anwar: Malaysia at turning point to shift from smart cities to AI cities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysia is now at a critical juncture to transition from Smart City development to Artificial Intelligence (AI) City development as the foundation for its aspiration to become an AI Nation, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said this was emphasised during a special meeting he chaired this morning at the Prime Minister’s Office to review and coordinate the implementation of an AI City model for Malaysia. Previously, Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said Malaysia is actively implementing the National AI Action Plan to achieve its aspiration of becoming an AI Nation by 2030.

items.title: Sack Rafizi? Anwar says focus on economy problem more important now
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today brushed aside questions over Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli’s position in the party, saying attention should instead be focused on managing the economy. Anwar, who is also prime minister and finance minister, was asked to respond to Rafizi’s challenge for PKR to sack him following a second show-cause letter over alleged breaches of party disciplinary and ethical rules. This morning Rafizi said he had received a second show-cause letter from disciplinary board chairman Datuk Zainol Samah, who alleged the Pandan MP had repeatedly made negative statements against the party and Anwar.

items.title: Langkawi ferry operator raises fares for non-MyKad holders by RM10 amid rising costs
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 10 — Langkawi Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd has announced a RM10 fare increase for non-MyKad passengers, effective April 15, 2026. After the adjustment, non-MyKad passenger fares from Kuala Perlis to Langkawi will be RM45 one way for adults and RM35 for children. Fares for Malaysian citizens remain unchanged at RM21 (adult) and RM16 (child) from Kuala Perlis, and RM26.50 (adult) and RM20.50 (child) from Kuala Kedah.

items.title: Paddy farmers seek stronger government support as costs rise, food security at risk
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 10 — Pertubuhan Persaudaraan Pesawah Malaysia (PeSAWAH), an organisation that represents and advocates paddy farmers, has called for stronger and sustained government support to help the group cope with rising production costs and ensure the sustainability of the country’s rice industry. Its chairman, Abdul Rashid Yob, said the increase in fuel prices has significantly impacted farm operating costs, necessitating a comprehensive approach to ensure the sector remains sustainable. Therefore, urgent government intervention is vital to support farmers and ensure the sustainability of the paddy industry,” he told reporters after a meeting session with farmers from Perlis, Penang and Perak here today.

items.title: Federal Court to hear PKR appeal over Zuraida’s RM100,000 bond ruling on July 16
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The Federal Court has fixed July 16 to hear Parti Keadilan Rakyat’s (PKR) appeal against a Court of Appeal decision that reduced former Ampang MP Datuk Zuraida Kamaruddin's bond payment from RM10 million to RM100,000 in a party bond breach case. On Tuesday, the Federal Court dismissed Zuraida's application to review its earlier decision granting PKR leave to appeal the appellate court's ruling. Earlier, on June 23, 2023, the Kuala Lumpur High Court ruled in favour of PKR and ordered Zuraida to pay RM10 million in accordance with the bond's terms.

items.title: EPF: Akaun Fleksibel withdrawals largely for short-term survival, but gap seen across income groups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysians tapping into their Employees Provident Fund (EPF) savings under the Akaun Fleksibel facility are largely using the money to cover daily necessities, healthcare and debt repayments rather than discretionary spending, according to the pension fund’s latest report. About 93 per cent used funds for daily necessities, 81.7 per cent for emergency-related expenses, 74.4 per cent for debt and investment obligations, and 73.3 per cent for seasonal or lifestyle costs. Health treatments accounted for 58.7 per cent, while debt payments represented 56.6 per cent.

items.title: MRT Corp to begin land acquisition for second segment of Penang LRT Mutiara Line
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 10 — MRT Corp will start the land acquisition process for the second segment of the Penang LRT Mutiara Line that runs between Macallum on the island and Penang Sentral in Butterworth, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook. He said a total of 38 private lands have been identified for acquisition. “A total of 61 privately owned land lots have completed the land hearing process under the Land Acquisition Act 1960,” he said.

items.title: Cheras karaoke brawl: Magistrate fines 34 men after guilty pleas in gang-linked attack
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Magistrates’ Court here today fined 34 men after they pled guilty to taking part in a violent riot at an entertainment centre in Cheras last week, an incident that involved firearms, explosives and other weapons. Two others, aged 19, were each fined RM2,000 in default three months’ jail. The court heard that the group was involved in a violent incident at a karaoke centre in Cheras at about 7.30pm on April 5, where they were found in possession of firearms, bullets, explosives, and weapons including sticks and stones that could be used to cause injury.

items.title: Anwar: Go easy on fuel as global energy shock drives up subsidy bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged Malaysians to use fuel more prudently as global energy pressures intensify and place growing strain on the government’s RON95 subsidy bill. He said the administration is trying to maintain the subsidised price of RON95 for now, even as rising international oil prices significantly increase fiscal costs. Anwar’s remarks come amid concerns over global energy market volatility, with the government previously assuring that current fuel stock levels remain sufficient in the near term.

items.title: Sungai Buloh escape: Prison Dept details breach during custody transfer, confirms joint hunt with police
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH, April 10 — The Prisons Department today confirmed that a remand detainee escaped from custody during a transfer process following court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court, prompting a nationwide manhunt. Police earlier identified the escapee as Muhammad Hassan, 24, a Pakistani remand detainee facing multiple serious charges, including sexual offences involving a child, robbery-related offences, and immigration violations. “The Malaysian Prisons Department has confirmed that a person accused escaped from police custody after the conclusion of court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court.

items.title: Anwar: One Malaysia-bound oil tanker breaks down in Hormuz, another has docked; no petrol supply impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — One oil tanker carrying Malaysian-bound crude from the Persian Gulf has broken down, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. In March, Anwar said he spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian about Malaysia’s “non-hostile” status and neutral foreign policy to secure an agreement for seven Malaysian-flagged vessels safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Roughly 38 per cent of Malaysia’s crude oil imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: APAD revokes licence of transport firm in Gemas trailer crash that killed three
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) has revoked the operator licence of MFG Barokah Enterprise, effective immediately, following the company’s involvement in a fatal trailer accident in Gemas last week that killed three people. In a statement, APAD said its investigation revealed the company had failed to comply with multiple licence conditions and safety requirements. The agency’s Licence Suspension and Revocation Committee met on April 9 and determined that MFG Barokah Enterprise had failed to adhere to the Industry Code of Practice (ICOP), ensure proper vehicle maintenance, and monitor its driver’s behaviour.

items.title: EPF: Nearly two-thirds of members haven’t used Akaun Fleksibel despite RM16.6b withdrawals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Some 63 per cent of Employees Provident Fund (EPF) members eligible for withdrawals under the Akaun Fleksibel facility have yet to tap into it, despite nearly RM16.6 billion already being withdrawn by others as of October 2025. Under the revised structure, contributions are split into three accounts: 75 per cent into Akaun Persaraan, 15 per cent into Akaun Sejahtera, and 10 per cent into Akaun Fleksibel. During the initial transfer period, which ended on August 31, 2024, some 4.1 million members moved RM14.5 billion into Akaun Fleksibel.

items.title: Four in 10 Malaysian room rentals across peninsula exclude at least one race, Architects of Diversity survey shows
items.summary: In the study titled The Room Rental Discrimination Vol. 2: The Semenanjung Report, AOD found that 43.6 per cent of 40,294 listings on rental platform iBilik included explicit racial preferences, making exclusion more common than listings open to all races. Overall, only 22.6 per cent of listings were open to all races, while 33.8 per cent did not state any preference.

items.title: Seventeen-year-old pleads not guilty to sexually assaulting boy, 10, at Batu Caves children’s welfare home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — A resident of a children’s welfare home pleaded not guilty at the Selayang Magistrate’s Court today to four charges of committing physical and non-physical sexual assault, as well as unnatural intercourse, against a boy. For the second charge, the teenager is accused of committing physical sexual assault against the same victim at the same place, time and date under Section 14(b) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, punishable under Section 14 of the same Act, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping. However, the owner of the welfare home, S. Valan, pleaded not guilty to four charges of physical sexual assault.

items.title: Lease idle farmland to boost food security, says agriculture minister
items.summary: LUNDU, April 10 — Owners of idle or underutilised agricultural land are urged to consider leasing their land to ensure it remains productive and continues contributing to the nation’s food supply. Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the initiative would help optimise land use while reducing Malaysia’s reliance on food imports. “Typically, companies seeking to lease land require long-term arrangements of 10 years or more, as agriculture takes time from planting to yield,” he said after visiting the Tanjung Purun padi planting project today.

items.title: Azalina: Second meeting on AG-prosecutor split examines key views
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Efforts to separate the roles of Attorney General (AG) and Public Prosecutor are being strengthened through engagement sessions with multiple parties, including non-governmental organisations and civil society. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, said at its second meeting yesterday, Parliament’s Special Select Committee examined various views, particularly on the appointment, termination and accountability of the Public Prosecutor “Yesterday I chaired the second meeting of the Special Select Committee on the separation of the AG and Public Prosecutor’s roles to advance institutional reform towards greater transparency and integrity,” she said in a Facebook post. She said engagement sessions will continue to ensure all views are considered in efforts to strengthen the nation’s justice system.

items.title: RM36m wildlife bust: Vietnamese man admits to possession of 1,000 python bile, 191 tongues after Johor Jaya raid
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 10 — A 39-year-old Vietnamese man pled guilty in the Sessions Court here today to possessing protected wildlife parts, including 1,022 python bile and 191 python tongues. The wildlife parts are listed as protected species under the First Schedule of the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716). Authorities previously reported that Perhilitan seized wildlife parts worth RM36.8 million in a raid at the same Taman Johor Jaya premises.

items.title: Zahid: Umno among Asean’s oldest parties as it turns 80
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said Umno, which is approaching its 80th anniversary, remains one of the oldest political parties in the Southeast Asian (Asean) region, with a legacy of struggle that reflects resilience and a key role in national development. The Deputy Prime Minister said that throughout its long history, Umno has undergone various phases of struggle and shifts in the political landscape, reflecting its experience, resilience and key role in national development. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, posted this in conjunction with Umno’s 80th anniversary.

items.title: Babysitter, mother claim trial to abusing four-year-old girl in Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — A babysitter and her mother pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here yesterday to a charge of abusing a four-year-old girl that resulted in physical injuries. A. Saranya, 28, and R. Saroja, 64, were jointly charged, as persons having care of the child, with abusing the victim, resulting in injuries to her thigh and genital area at their residence in Jalan Pudu Impian here between February 2026 and April 3, 2026. Deputy public prosecutor Ravindejit Kaur suggested bail be set at RM30,000 each with one surety, however, lawyer Mohammad Arifin Abdul Wahab, representing the two accused, asked for a lower bail amount, saying Saranya is nine months pregnant and due to deliver her sixth child this month, while her mother, who is unemployed, suffers from hypertension and high cholesterol.
